title: Skírnismál
stanzas: The lord of elves saw a maiden fair,
In the high seat of the gods so bright,
Her beauty shone like the morning sun,
And his heart grew heavy with love and pain.
He sent his servant to seek a bride,
To find the maiden and win her love,
But she dwelt far in the giants' land,
Where the sun shines not and the ice grows thick.
The servant rode on the steed so swift,
Through forests dark and over mountains high,
At last he came to the giant's dwelling,
Where Gerd the maiden kept her home.
He spoke to her of Skirnir's love,
And offered gifts of gold and power,
But she would not hear his pleading words,
And turned away with proud disdain.
Then he threatened her with the runes so dire,
With the curse of age and wasting sickness,
He told her of spells that freeze the heart,
And make the days stretch long and drear.
At last she yielded to his dark words,
And promised to meet her lover bold,
In the sacred grove where the gods have met,
When nine nights hence the moon shall come.
When Skirnir returned with the happy news,
The lord of elves grew filled with joy,
He gave his sword to the faithful slave,
But without it he would die in the fight.
At Ragnarok when the great tree shakes,
Skirnir shall have no sword to wield,
But the love he won through the servant's wit,
Shall live forever in the songs of men.
